name: Bottom-Up DP
summary: Fill a table where dp[i] = dp[i-1] + dp[i-2]; each stair's ways come from the two stairs below it.
visualizationIntent: init: build the dp table (all zeros), then seed dp[1]=1 and dp[2]=2 with a write flash. loop: a pointer i walks left-to-right from index 3; at each step the two source cells dp[i-1] and dp[i-2] flash compared (blue) and the current cell dp[i] fills with their sum and flashes (the recurrence dp[i]=dp[i-1]+dp[i-2] made visible). return: the final cell dp[n] is marked result (green) — the answer. dp[0] stays dimmed (unused).

lineExplanations.3: Make a table with one slot per stair, 0 through n.
lineExplanations.4: If the staircase has at least one stair...
lineExplanations.5: There is exactly 1 way to stand on stair 1.
lineExplanations.6: If the staircase has at least two stairs...
lineExplanations.7: There are exactly 2 ways to reach stair 2 (1+1, or 2).
lineExplanations.8: Fill stairs 3 through n in increasing order.
lineExplanations.9: Ways to reach stair i = ways to (i-1) plus ways to (i-2).
lineExplanations.10: The answer is the number of ways to reach the top stair n.
lineExplanations.11: Return the total number of distinct climbs.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution(object): the wrapper class LeetCode expects.
syntaxExplanations.2: def climbStairs(self, n): the entry method; n is the number of stairs.
syntaxExplanations.3: [0] * (n + 1) makes a list of n+1 zeros (indices 0..n).
syntaxExplanations.5: dp[1] = 1 seeds the first base case.
syntaxExplanations.7: dp[2] = 2 seeds the second base case.
syntaxExplanations.8: for i in range(3, n + 1): loops i from 3 up to n.
syntaxExplanations.9: dp[i] = dp[i - 1] + dp[i - 2] applies the recurrence.
syntaxExplanations.10: answer = dp[n] reads the top stair's count.
syntaxExplanations.11: return answer hands back the total.
